---
title: "Email a Decision Maker"
subtitle: "Send a message to the decision-makers who have the power to make Pacific Coast Highway safer."
---

```{=html}
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&family=Playfair+Display:wght@700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet" />
```


Personal messages from community members matter. Use the form below to representatives and call for immediate action to protect cyclists, pedestrians, and everyone who travels this corridor. You can use the provided template or add your own story, connection to PCH, and why safer infrastructure matters to you.

When you click Send Email, your default email app will open with the message ready to send.


```{=html}
<div class="email-box">

  <div class="email-intro">
    <strong>Who receives this email?</strong>
    This message goes to a local city decision maker working on transportation and street safety in Ventura.
    Your voice helps prioritize safer infrastructure for cyclists and pedestrians.
  </div>

  <div class="form-row">
    <div class="form-group">
      <label for="sender-name">Your Name</label>
      <input type="text" id="sender-name" placeholder="Jane Smith" />
    </div>
    <div class="form-group">
      <label for="sender-hood">Your Neighborhood <em style="font-weight:400;text-transform:none">(optional)</em></label>
      <input type="text" id="sender-hood" placeholder="e.g. Midtown, Downtown, Montalvo" />
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="form-group">
    <label for="email-subject">Subject Line</label>
    <input type="text" id="email-subject" value="URGENT: PCH Safety Crisis — Two Cyclists Killed June 11 Near Solimar Beach, Ventura County" />
  </div>

  <div class="form-group">
    <label for="email-body">Your Message</label>
    <textarea id="email-body" oninput="updateCount()">Honorable Representative,
    
We are writing to you with deep urgency and grief on behalf of our community — residents, cyclists, recreational users, and workers who live and travel throughout Ventura County and the greater tri-county area.

On the evening of June 11, 2026, two people were killed and a third seriously injured on Pacific Coast Highway (State Route 1) near Solimar Beach Road, just north of Ventura. According to the California Highway Patrol and the Ventura County District Attorney's Office, a 24-year-old Oxnard man was traveling at 50–55 mph when he veered his pickup truck into the designated bicycle lane and struck three cyclists from behind. Kellie Standish, 33, of Bend, Oregon, died at the scene. William Colby Tucker, 39, of Ventura, died at Ventura County Medical Center. A third cyclist was hospitalized with serious injuries. The driver was arrested at the scene and has since been charged with two counts of second-degree murder, DUI causing injury, and driving on a suspended license. He is being held without bail.

We write to you today about a separate and independent matter, one that predates June 11 and will outlast it: the documented, chronic failure of the PCH corridor in Ventura County to protect the lives of cyclists and pedestrians.

This is not a problem that began with one driver's choice. According to crash data documented in the California SWITRS database, maintained by the California Highway Patrol and mapped by UC Berkeley's SafeTREC through the TIMS platform, this stretch of State Route 1 has seen at least 11 fatalities and countless serious injuries over recent years across a range of crash types and contributing factors, not only impairment. The infrastructure of this corridor does not require a drunk driver to kill people. It has done so without one. That is the independent public safety crisis we are asking you to address.

This stretch of highway operates at a posted speed limit of 45 mph, with vehicles routinely exceeding that speed. It carries painted bike lanes with no physical separation from high-speed vehicle traffic. It provides beach access points, campgrounds, and recreational entry used by millions of visitors annually across the tri-county region. The infrastructure signals to cyclists, pedestrians, and families that this is a corridor where they are welcome. That signal is not backed by a design that protects their lives.

Painted lines are not protection. They are paint.
We are calling on you to act on the independent, systemic infrastructure failures of this corridor. Specifically, we respectfully urge you to:
- Immediately convene a multi-agency safety summit involving Caltrans District 7, CHP, Ventura County, and local municipalities to assess the full scope of fatalities and serious injuries on PCH between the Los Angeles County line and the City of Ventura — across all crash types and contributing factors.

- Direct Caltrans to conduct an expedited safety study of the Ventura County PCH corridor, separate from and in addition to the ongoing Malibu-focused PCH Master Plan, with specific focus on the stretch near Solimar Beach, Faria Beach, Rincon, and Emma Wood State Beach.

- Pursue emergency protective infrastructure measures in the near term, including physical separation barriers between bicycle lanes and vehicle travel lanes, reduced speed limits through known recreational access zones, enhanced signage, and increased DUI and speed enforcement.

- Explore long-term solutions including dedicated separated bicycle paths, physical medians, and road design that honestly reflects the multi-modal nature of this corridor.

- Secure state and federal funding — including ATP, HSIP, and CMAQ program resources — for safety improvements on this stretch of PCH, which serves both urban and unincorporated communities across county lines.

- Engage directly with affected community members, cyclists, and active transportation advocates in Ventura County in designing any proposed safety solutions.

We acknowledge that improvements to a state highway require coordination across multiple levels of government, and we recognize the complexity of that process. But the families of Kellie Standish and William Colby Tucker, and the families of the others killed on this corridor before them, deserve more than condolences. They deserve to know that their loved ones' deaths will prompt lasting, structural change — independent of the outcome of any criminal proceeding.

We are unequivocal: the driver who chose to drive while impaired and who had previously been warned of the deadly consequences of doing so following a December 2025 DUI arrest bears sole criminal responsibility for the deaths of Kellie and Colby. Nothing in this letter should be read as diminishing that responsibility or as commentary on the pending criminal proceedings. Justice for Kellie and Colby must be pursued fully and without compromise.

We are community members who ride, run, walk, live, and work along the Central Coast. We are not asking for the road to be closed to cars. We are asking you to treat this corridor as the public safety emergency the data shows it to be and to act accordingly.
Thank you for your attention to this critical matter. We welcome the opportunity to meet with your office and with Caltrans to discuss solutions. We stand ready to engage.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]</textarea>
    <div class="char-hint" id="char-count">0 characters</div>
  </div>

  <button class="send-btn" onclick="sendEmail()">✉ Send Email to Decision Maker</button>
  <p class="email-note">
    Clicking "Send Email" opens your default email application with this message pre-filled —
    you can review it before sending. Nothing is sent automatically.
  </p>
</div>

<script>
function updateCount() {
  const len = document.getElementById("email-body").value.length;
  document.getElementById("char-count").textContent = len.toLocaleString() + " characters";
}

function sendEmail() {
  const name    = document.getElementById("sender-name").value.trim();
  const hood    = document.getElementById("sender-hood").value.trim();
  const subject = document.getElementById("email-subject").value.trim();
  let   body    = document.getElementById("email-body").value.trim();

  // Insert name into signature placeholder
  const sig = name ? (hood ? name + ", " + hood + " resident" : name) : "[Your Name]";
  body = body.replace("[Your Name]", sig);

  const mailto = "mailto:saferecventura@gmail.com"
    + "?subject=" + encodeURIComponent(subject)
    + "&body="    + encodeURIComponent(body);

  window.location.href = mailto;
}

// Init count
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", updateCount);
</script>
```

---
